title: Phase 4: Assess alignment of each UI with the real goal

evaluations.name: Metadata Co-pilot Workbench (frontend/)
evaluations.verdict: Strongest UI. Should be the foundation for the unified UI.

evaluations.name: QA Tool (app/ui_qa/)
evaluations.verdict: Valuable QA infrastructure with code quality issues. Unique features should be preserved.

evaluations.name: Chat UI (app/ui_chat/)
evaluations.verdict: Right idea, wrong platform. Conversational discovery should migrate to React.

deadFeatures: index command referenced in CLAUDE.md but not implemented
evaluations.name: CLI (app/cli.py)
uniqueFeatures: MARC XML parsing (only UI exposing M1)
evaluations.verdict: Solid developer tool. Keep as developer/ops interface.

deadFeatures: Entire tool duplicated in QA Tool
doNotSurvive: Duplicate in QA Tool should be removed
evaluations.name: QA Regression Runner (app/qa.py)
overlappingFeatures: Regression testing (QA Tool Gold Set page)
evaluations.verdict: Essential but duplicated. Should be canonical regression runner.

evaluations.name: FastAPI Backend (app/api/)
evaluations.verdict: Correct architectural center but growing complex. Needs decomposition and caching.

uis.description: React SPA with 4 routes (/, /workbench, /agent, /review). Proxies /metadata/* to FastAPI backend. Has pre-built dist/ but no production serving config. ~3,160 lines of TypeScript/TSX.
uis.name: Metadata Co-pilot Workbench
uis.purpose: Agent-driven HITL system for improving bibliographic metadata quality. 4 pages: Dashboard, Workbench, Agent Chat, Review.
uis.techStack: React 19, TypeScript, Vite 8, Tailwind CSS 4, TanStack Query 5, TanStack Table 8, Recharts 3, React Router 7

uis.description: Multi-page Streamlit app with 7 page files. Calls QueryService in-process (no FastAPI dependency). Has own SQLite database at data/qa/qa.db. Includes guided wizard workflow, DB explorer, and 15 canonical test queries. ~3,530 lines of Python.
uis.name: QA Tool
uis.purpose: Quality assurance for M4 query pipeline: execute queries, label results as TP/FP/FN, track issues, export gold sets for regression testing. 6 visible pages plus 1 hidden wizard.

uis.description: Single-page Streamlit chat app that calls FastAPI /chat endpoint via HTTP. Has dedicated launcher script (run_chat_ui.sh) that starts both API and UI. Primo links configured for Tel Aviv University. ~450 lines of Python.
uis.entryPoint: poetry run streamlit run app/ui_chat/main.py or ./run_chat_ui.sh (http://localhost:8501)
uis.name: Chat UI
uis.purpose: Conversational discovery interface for searching the rare books collection. Chat-style with message history, follow-up suggestions, expandable results with Primo catalog links.

uis.description: 5 commands: parse-marc, query, chat-init, chat-history, chat-cleanup. Calls scripts directly in-process. Stale documentation in app/README.md. ~334 lines.
uis.name: CLI
uis.purpose: Command-line interface for MARC pipeline operations: parse MARC XML, execute queries, manage chat sessions.

uis.description: CLI companion to the QA Tool. Uses exit codes 0/1 for CI integration. Gold sets are exported from the QA Tool UI. ~187 lines.
uis.name: QA Regression Runner
uis.purpose: Automated regression testing against gold set expectations. Validates that query results match expected includes/excludes.

uis.description: Two modules: main.py (chat/session/health/websocket endpoints, ~1,130 lines) and metadata.py (12 metadata quality endpoints, ~1,459 lines). Auto-generates Swagger UI at /docs and ReDoc at /redoc. ~3,024 lines total.
uis.name: FastAPI Backend (API Layer)
uis.purpose: Central HTTP API serving the Chat UI and Metadata Workbench. 17 REST endpoints plus 1 WebSocket. Also provides auto-generated Swagger UI and ReDoc documentation.
uis.targetUsers: Frontend clients (React Workbench, Streamlit Chat UI), developers (Swagger/ReDoc)
uis.techStack: FastAPI 0.115, Pydantic, Uvicorn, slowapi (rate limiting), CORS middleware

projectGoal.centerpiece: The two-phase conversational query engine: intent interpretation with confidence scoring -> corpus exploration with aggregation, refinement, and enrichment, delivered through the chatbot UI
coreUseCases: Natural language bibliographic query with evidence-based results traceable to MARC fields
Two-phase conversational exploration: define a result set with confidence scoring, then aggregate and analyze it
Multi-turn refinement and follow-up within a session
Normalization quality improvement via HITL metadata co-pilot
Query pipeline regression testing via gold sets
noise: Duplicate regression runner in QA Tool
primaryUsers: Bibliographic researchers and scholars who need to query rare book collections in natural language and trust the results
Metadata librarians who maintain normalization quality and authority records
Developers who validate query pipeline correctness
projectGoal.projectGoal: Enable scholars and librarians to discover rare books through natural language conversation, backed by a deterministic, evidence-based query engine over MARC XML bibliographic records with full provenance tracking.
secondary: Metadata Co-pilot Workbench -- data quality admin for catalogers
QA Tool -- query pipeline validation and regression testing
Enrichment pipeline -- external data augmentation, partially integrated
CLI -- developer access to the M1-M4 pipeline

inputs.prompt: Deep product, UX, architecture, and implementation evaluation of all existing UIs to support design and build of a single new integrated UI for Rare Books Bot - beta version. The bot is the heart of the system. The new UI must include observability and debugging. Preferred direction: React or Angular.
